title: Advertising accounts managers and creative directors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate advertising policies and programs or produce collateral materials, such as posters, contests, coupons, or giveaways, to create extra interest in the purchase of a product or service for a department, an entire organization, or on an account basis. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities to solicit and maintain funds for special projects or nonprofit organizations. (Based on 7 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare budgets and submit estimates for program costs as part of campaign plan development.
Coordinate with the media to disseminate advertising.
Plan and prepare advertising and promotional material to increase sales of products or services, working with customers, company officials, sales departments, and advertising agencies.
Analyze the effectiveness of marketing tactics or channels and monitor industry statistics and trends to forecast future conditions.

Conduct research on consumer opinions, buying habits, and identify target audiences for products, services, or technologies, including conducting market research analysis and gathering data on competitors.
Coordinate with marketing team members, graphic artists, and other workers to develop and implement marketing programs, including preparing and negotiating advertising and sales contracts.
Develop comprehensive marketing strategies and collaborate with other marketing staff to integrate and complement marketing strategies across multiple sales channels.
Direct, motivate, and monitor the mobilization of a campaign team to advance campaign goals, including establishing goals for soliciting funds and developing fundraising activity plans.
Execute and manage social media campaigns, and conduct online marketing initiatives, such as paid ad placement and email promotions, to enhance advertising efforts.
Plan and execute advertising policies and strategies for organizations, including evaluating advertising and promotion programs for compatibility with fundraising efforts.

Identify and develop contacts for promotional campaigns and industry programs, coordinate activities with departments such as sales and research, and track program budgets and expenses to evaluate campaigns.
Inspect layouts and advertising copy, and edit scripts, audio, video, and other promotional material for adherence to specifications while discussing product themes and methods with clients.
Develop and implement procedures for identifying advertising needs and manage sales team performance, including training and direction.
Compile or develop materials to submit to granting or other funding organizations, including conducting research to identify potential donors.
Create content strategies for digital media and write a range of informative, marketing, and promotional material, including press releases and articles.
Keep abreast of government regulations and emerging Web technology to ensure compliance, and attend conferences or promotional events related to products.
Facilitate consumer relations and improve relationships between different parts of the company, such as managers, employees, and branch offices.
